headline: CATTLE RALLY WHILE CRUDE CRASHES 6%
subheadline: Feeders flip leadership as oil's collapse eases input costs.
lead: Cattle rallied to $252.93 as feeders took the lead at $372.23, up 1.4% — the first time feeders outpaced live cattle since the breakdown began. That's not momentum, that's structure change. Oil's 6% crash to $94.54 removes the input cost pressure that's been weighing on the complex for three weeks. The buyer isn't gone, just waiting for cleaner entry.
the_takeaway: Feeder strength flips the script — breakdown thesis needs new evidence.
teaser: Feeders lead cattle higher as oil crashes remove cost pressure.

one_number.context: Oil's biggest single-day drop in two months removes the input cost pressure that's been weighing on cattle margins. When diesel falls this hard, feedlot economics improve overnight. The timing isn't coincidence — cattle buyers were waiting for energy to roll over before stepping back in.

yesterdays_call.summary: Called cattle bounce real but thin, with feeder weakness keeping breakdown thesis alive.
yesterdays_call.note: Feeders flipped to leading higher today — the conditional resolved cleanly.

sections.title: Cattle Find Real Buyers
sections.body: Live cattle pushed to $252.93 while feeders jumped 1.4% to $372.23 — the first session feeders outran live cattle since the April selloff began. That's not technical bounce, that's structural shift. Packers bought aggressively across regions, driving futures and cash to new session highs. The feeder strength tells the story: when the leading indicator turns, the complex follows. Next resistance sits at $255, but the momentum shifted today.
sections.catalyst: Cattle futures reach record highs as packers aggressively bought cattle
sections.bottom_line: Structure changed — feeders leading higher breaks the breakdown pattern.
sections.vs_yesterday: Cattle: thin bounce became structural shift as feeders took leadership

sections.title: Oil Crash Lifts Margins
sections.body: Crude crashed 6% to $94.54 in the biggest single-day drop since March, removing the input cost squeeze that's pressured livestock margins for three weeks. Diesel follows crude with a lag, but feedlots pencil energy costs months forward. The cattle rally isn't coincidence — buyers were waiting for this energy rollover. Natural gas slid 1.7% to $2.73, adding to the margin relief. Feed costs matter more than headline grain prices when energy moves this hard.
sections.catalyst: WTI crude oil crashes 6% in biggest drop since March
sections.bottom_line: Energy collapse removes the cost pressure weighing on livestock margins.

sections.title: Grains Drift Lower
sections.body: Corn slid 1.1% to $4.72 as swift planting progress keeps weather premium minimal. Beans eased 0.3% to $12.06 with managed money still long but rotating into energy. Wheat fell 1.8% to $6.13 on no specific catalyst — looks like fund liquidation as dollars flow toward oversold crude. The grain complex is marking time until the next weather scare or export surprise. Planting pace above seasonal average removes urgency from the May premium.
sections.catalyst: Swift plantings muddy grain prices as progress stays ahead of seasonal
sections.bottom_line: No weather premium with planting on pace — funds rotating to energy.

outside_the_pit.title: Tyson reports loss in beef business amid packer criticism
outside_the_pit.body: Timing's not subtle: same day Agriculture Secretary Rollins criticized packer concentration, Tyson announced beef losses. The structural margin squeeze at the packer level explains why cattle prices can rally even when retail beef stays expensive. Market concentration debates heat up when packers lose money too.

outside_the_pit.title: USDA and Export-Import Bank expand ag export partnership
outside_the_pit.body: Historic partnership aims to boost farm exports while reducing the agricultural trade deficit. Export financing gets easier when the government backs the deals. Watch for this to show up in quarterly export commitments — grain exporters get cheaper capital to compete with South America.

outside_the_pit.title: Bird flu vaccine escape mutations identified in new research
outside_the_pit.body: Scientists mapped which genetic changes help H5 viruses evade current vaccines. The research comes as USDA reviews new screwworm control technology. Animal health pipeline stays critical when disease pressure builds — prevention costs less than containment.

spread_to_watch.label: Live cattle / feeder ratio
spread_to_watch.commentary: When this ratio tightens, feeders are gaining on live cattle — exactly what happened today. The 1.4% feeder rally versus 0.4% live cattle gain narrows the spread and signals buying interest down the supply chain. Tight ratios historically lead broader cattle rallies.

basis.headline: Midwest cattle basis firms as packers compete for supplies
basis.body: Cash cattle trade reset higher across the Plains as packers bid aggressively for current supplies. Basis levels tightened from Texas to Nebraska as the packer competition that drove futures higher showed up in local markets too. Eastern feedlots seeing the strongest basis improvement — closer to processing centers means less freight drag when margins tighten.

weekly_thread.question: Will the cattle complex find support at $250 or continue the breakdown through key technical levels?
weekly_thread.status_text: Feeders answered the question today — leading higher breaks the breakdown pattern that required feeder weakness. The $250 floor held and structure shifted.

the_more_you_know.title: Why 1.4% feeder strength changes everything
the_more_you_know.body: Today's 1.4% feeder rally to $372.23 matters more than live cattle's 0.4% gain because feeders are the leading indicator. When the feeder/live ratio tightens, it signals buying interest down the supply chain — replacement cattle cost more relative to finished cattle. That structural shift historically precedes broader cattle rallies by 2-3 weeks. The breakdown thesis required feeder weakness to validate. Today's leadership flip removes that pillar.

watch_list.desc: Weekly export sales — corn under 800K MT keeps funds rotating out
May cattle options expiry — $250 puts expire worthless if close holds
WASDE report — first summer outlook for old-crop supplies
Crude below $90 would accelerate livestock margin relief

daily_quote.text: Land is a community is the basic concept of ecology, but that land is to be loved and respected is an extension of ethics.

source_summary: CME Group, USDA, energy markets, agricultural trade publications
